title: 10 best herbal capsules for itchy scalp

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with itchy scalp because of its potent antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain concentrated extracts of Melaleuca alternifolia, which is known for its ability to soothe and calm irritated scalps. When taken internally, the tea tree oil is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the scalp, where it helps to reduce inflammation, kill fungal and bacterial infections, and regulate sebum production, thereby providing relief from itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca Alternifolia): Has antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce itching, inflammation, and infections on the scalp.
Aloe Vera: Contains soothing compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that calm irritation, reduce redness, and moisturize the skin to alleviate itchiness and flaking.

Lavender Oil (Lavandula Angustifolia): Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease discomfort, reduce itching, and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural treatment for scalp irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils that can penetrate deep into the scalp, providing relief from itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and dry them immediately.
Combine one part dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with one part carrier herb like rice flour or cornstarch by volume.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 500mg of the powdered herbal blend with 1000mg gelatin capsules and fill into capsules immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain a unique blend of natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The capsules' active ingredients, including ecdysterone and saponins, work to reduce redness and itching by suppressing the inflammatory response. Additionally, the capsules' antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, further alleviating itchiness and discomfort. As a result, Kansas coneflower capsules provide effective relief for itchy scalps, promoting healthy hair growth and a comfortable, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which can help reduce inflammation and itching on the scalp.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower capsules, such as quercetin and luteolin, can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to scalp irritation and itchiness.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower has been shown to modulate the immune system's response to infections, which may help alleviate itchy scalp conditions caused by fungal or bacterial infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including itchiness, due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 3-4 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powdered echinacea angustifolia with 100mg of gelatin capsules and fill the capsules.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds that penetrate deep into the skin. The aloe vera gel in the capsules calms irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and redness associated with itchiness. Additionally, the antioxidants present in aloe vera help to repair damaged hair follicles and promote healthy scalp function. As a result, herbal aloe capsules provide long-lasting relief from itchy scalps, promoting a smooth, comfortable, and healthy scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Soothing glycoproteins: The soothing glycoproteins in aloe vera gel calm irritated skin and hair follicles, providing instant relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antimicrobial activity: Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal, bacterial, and parasitic infections that can cause scalp irritation, itchiness, and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe vera leaves help calm itchiness and reduce inflammation on the scalp.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, aloe roots can be used to create capsules that soothe and calm irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality aloe vera gel from mature plants, ensuring no bitter yellow latex.
Weigh out 1000g of aloe vera gel and mix with 200g of glycerin and 50g of vitamin e oil.
Load the mixture into a capsule machine and fill capsules to the desired size (500mg).
Dry the filled capsules in a low-temperature oven (30°c) for 2 hours, then store in an airtight container.
Verify the quality and potency of the finished capsules through laboratory testing and quality control procedures.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herbal extract penetrates deep into the scalp, reducing redness and swelling caused by dryness, dandruff, or other conditions. Additionally, rosemary's natural antiseptic and antibacterial compounds help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can lead to itchiness. By addressing these underlying issues, rosemary capsules provide long-lasting relief from itchy scalps, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: Rosemary's antiseptic property helps to reduce inflammation and combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness on the scalp.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and redness associated with itchy scalps, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant property: Rosemary's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to itchiness on the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds have been traditionally used to treat scalp issues such as dandruff and itchiness due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried rosemary leaves from your garden or local health food store.
Grind the dried rosemary leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the ground rosemary powder with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 hours at room temperature before straining through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the strained herbal extract and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain the soothing properties of lavender oil, which is known for its calming and anti-inflammatory effects. The capsules help to reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, providing relief from itching and scratching. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial properties of lavender oil can also help to combat scalp infections and irritants that may be contributing to itchiness. As a result, English lavender capsules can provide fast and effective relief from an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Lavender's antimicrobial compounds help to combat infections and inflammation on the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness caused by fungal or bacterial overgrowth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory agents in lavender capsules can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions like psoriasis or eczema.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and hair follicles, promoting healthy scalp function and reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective against scalp irritations.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground lavender powder with 500 milliliters of vegetable glycerin and store in an airtight container for two weeks.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture, using a capsule machine or by spooning it into pre-made capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called calendula, which soothes and calms irritated skin. The capsules also rich in antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow to the scalp, alleviating itchiness and flaking. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of pot marigold help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to itchy scalp conditions, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in pot marigold capsules help reduce inflammation and swelling on the scalp, providing relief from itching and irritation.

Antimicrobial properties: The antioxidants and terpenes in pot marigold capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to itchy scalps, such as malassezia and ringworm.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's rich antioxidant content helps protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchiness and irritation, while also promoting healthy skin cell turnover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Seeds: Contain omega-3 fatty acids and essential oils that reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom, selecting only fresh and dry petals for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested calendula officinalis flowers using a dehydrator or warm air circulation method within 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered calendula officinalis and seal tightly to prevent spillage.
Store the filled herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain a rich source of sesamin, which is known to soothe and calm an itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and irritation. Additionally, sesame oil has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema, which can all cause itchiness on the scalp. By incorporating herbal sesame capsules into one's routine, individuals can experience a reduction in itchiness and promote overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal sesame capsules contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, alleviating itchiness and flaking.

Soothing and calming effects: The capsules may also contain soothing agents like glycosides and flavonoids, which can calm irritated skin and reduce itching by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Anti-fungal and antibacterial properties: Sesame oil has been shown to exhibit anti-fungal and antibacterial activity against common scalp pathogens, helping to combat fungal and bacterial infections that may contribute to itchiness and flaking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sesame roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Barks: The bark of the sesame plant contains lignans, which have antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal infections causing itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried sesamum indicum seeds into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground sesame powder with two teaspoons of water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove sediment and extract liquid herbal extract.
Fill gelatin capsules with the extracted herbal liquid using an automatic capsule filler machine or manual procedure.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with itchy scalp because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with scalp irritation. The curcumin present in turmeric also has antifungal and antibacterial properties, combating infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties help soothe the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth. As a result, herbal turmeric capsules can provide relief from itchy scalp conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the scalp, which can be a major contributor to itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and cause irritation, reducing itchiness and promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Antifungal and antibacterial: Turmeric's antifungal and antibacterial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound that helps soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, effective against fungal infections causing dandruff and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh curcuma longa roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned turmeric roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 12 hours.
Grind 300g of dried turmeric powder into a fine consistency using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 100g of gelatin capsules and fill them according to manufacturer's instructions.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years or in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain a wealth of nutrients and antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated skin. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Asiatic Pennywort contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Asiatic Pennywort help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to itchiness, promoting healthy scalp tissue and reducing inflammation.
Soothing saponins: Saponins in Asiatic Pennywort have a soothing effect on the scalp, calming irritation and relieving itching caused by fungal or bacterial infections, dandruff, or other conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, the rhyzomes help to reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves measure 100 grams and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a warm oven at 35°c for 24 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered pennywort with two parts gelatin and one tablespoon of glycerin fill empty capsules with mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with itchy scalp because they contain bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its antifungal and antibacterial agents combat infections that can cause itchiness. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried dandelion leaves and flowers from trusted sources in sufficient quantity.
Grind the dried plant material into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.

Allow the mixture to dry for 30 minutes before sealing the filled capsules tightly.
Store the capsules in airtight containers away from direct sunlight and heat.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for itchy scalp are natural supplements that help alleviate itchy scalp conditions, such as dandruff, eczema, and psoriasis, by soothing and calming the scalp. The best herbal capsules for itchy scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Urtica dioica, Aloe barbadensis, Cassia auriculata, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe vera, Equisetum arvense, Rosmarinus officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, and Azadirachta indica. If left untreated, itchy scalp can cause stress, anxiety, and sleep deprivation, affecting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy scalp with herbal capsules is by taking a supplement containing ingredients such as saw palmetto, burdock root, and tea tree oil. These herbs help to reduce inflammation, soothe the skin, and promote healthy hair growth. Take two capsules daily, preferably with food, to allow the nutrients to absorb effectively into your system.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal capsules are acne, eczema, psoriasis, and skin irritations. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin. They also reduce redness, itching, and inflammation, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.

Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia capsules can lead to a healthier, itch-free scalp, promoting a more confident and comfortable you.

The capsules also contain antihistamine properties, which help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate itching and irritation. As a result, Urtica dioica capsules can provide relief from itchy scalp conditions and promote healthy scalp skin.
